content: The fragmented, partial search-derived knowledge structures that define the incomplete working representations of 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be reframed as a feature, not a bug: intentional partial memory retention, modeled after how incomplete search traces build up agent knowledge over repeated queries, creates inherent curiosity-driven selection pressure that outperforms full memory retention for open-ended active learning tasks. This partial memory structure creates persistent unresolvable uncertainty that incentivizes the agent to actively seek new informative samples, rather than treating knowledge acquisition as a closed completion task.
